name: Satisfied
description: A state of emotional or psychological contentment.
example: I finished my study goal for today, and I felt satisfied.

name: Happy
description: A pleasant response when things feel fulfilling, comfortable, and good.
example: After school, I ate snacks and chatted with my close friends, and I felt happy.

name: Blessed
description: A warm feeling of joy, contentment, and comfort, as if worries have drifted away.
example: After school, I spent time with my good friends, and I felt blessed and happy.

name: Proud
description: Feeling confident and satisfied because of your own effort or achievement.
example: My teacher praised my presentation in class, and I felt proud and happy.

name: Excited
description: A feeling full of anticipation and energy that makes joy hard to hide.
example: I got the new game I had been waiting for, and I felt so excited to play it.

name: Exhilarated
description: Feeling free, open, and refreshed when you do something with full enjoyment.
example: During the holiday, I sang and danced loudly, and it felt exhilarating.

name: Ecstatic
description: An intense feeling of excitement and joy that sends your emotions soaring.
example: I got a perfect score on the test, and I jumped up in pure ecstasy.

name: Expectant
description: Feeling full of hope and excitement about something good that may happen in the future.
example: Summer vacation was coming soon, and I eagerly planned all the fun things I could do.

name: Confident
description: A steady belief that you can succeed or overcome difficulties.
example: Because I had prepared well, I felt confident about doing well in the speech contest.

name: Interested
description: Feeling curious and enthusiastic about something, and wanting to spend time and effort on it.
example: I am very interested in drawing, so I spend hours on it after school every day.

name: Longing
description: A strong inner desire to gain, do, or achieve something.
example: I long to find my own interests and passions so life can feel joyful and meaningful.

name: Comfortable
description: Feeling physically and emotionally at ease, relaxed, and pleasant.
example: Lying down on a cozy bed to rest made me feel very comfortable.

name: Relaxed
description: Feeling calm in your mind and released from tension in your body.
example: Lying on the grass and listening to the wind in the trees helped me feel relaxed.

name: Calm
description: A peaceful state of mind without worries, bringing steadiness and comfort.
example: I sat down, took deep breaths, and let the calm atmosphere ease my stress.

name: Reassured
description: Feeling safe, settled, and free from worry.
example: Being at home with my family around me made me feel reassured and warm.

name: At Ease
description: Feeling light, natural, and free from pressure or tension.
example: Playing games and laughing with my best friends made me feel completely at ease.

name: Trusting
description: A steady belief that someone is honest and reliable, allowing you to feel safe with them.
example: I shared my feelings with a friend and felt trusting because I knew I would be understood.

name: Shy
description: Feeling uneasy when facing other people or unfamiliar situations.
example: In a new class, I felt shy and did not dare to talk to my classmates first.

name: Worried
description: Feeling troubled or uneasy about a particular situation or outcome.
example: I was worried that the weather tomorrow would be bad and affect our outdoor activity.

name: Lost
description: Feeling confused and helpless about a situation or choice, not knowing what to do next.
example: When I thought about my future school path, I felt lost and did not know what choice was right.

name: Uneasy
description: Feeling anxious and unsettled about something unknown or risky.
example: I felt uneasy before the exam because I was not sure whether I could do well.

name: Afraid
description: Feeling anxious and timid when facing a threat or an unsafe situation.
example: I was home alone at night and felt afraid, so I turned on every light.

name: Nervous
description: Feeling anxious before an important moment, often with physical reactions like a racing heart or sweaty hands.
example: Before the competition, I felt nervous and my palms started sweating.

name: Anxious
description: Feeling worried, tense, and uneasy about a possible bad outcome.
example: As the final report deadline got closer, everyone felt anxious about not finishing in time.

name: Terrified
description: A strong fear caused by real or imagined danger or threat.
example: Walking down a dark and unfamiliar alley made me feel terrified that something bad might happen.

name: Stunned
description: Feeling shocked and unable to react after something unexpected happens.
example: When I was chosen to be class leader, I felt stunned because I had never expected it.

name: Surprised
description: Feeling startled or amazed because something happens unexpectedly.
example: Xiao-juan loves desserts, so I was surprised when she gave me her cake.

name: Pleasantly Surprised
description: Feeling excited and happy because of an unexpected event or situation.
example: The surprise birthday party made me feel pleasantly surprised and delighted.

name: Shocked
description: Feeling intensely surprised by something extreme or hard to believe.
example: Hearing about the severe earthquake damage in the country left everyone shocked.

name: Helplessly Resigned
description: Feeling there is nothing you can do to change or solve the situation.
example: When it suddenly started raining, I felt helplessly resigned because I had not brought any rain gear.

name: Frustrated
description: Feeling upset and discouraged after facing obstacles, setbacks, or difficulty.
example: I tried several times and still could not solve the hard problem, so I felt frustrated.

name: Disappointed
description: Feeling sad or dissatisfied because things did not turn out as hoped.
example: The event I had been looking forward to was canceled at the last minute, and I felt disappointed.

name: Powerless
description: Feeling unable to influence, solve, or change a situation because you lack ability, resources, or control.
example: I tried to resolve the conflict, but the other person would not cooperate, so I felt powerless.

name: Discouraged
description: Feeling emotionally low after setbacks, obstacles, or unmet goals.
example: I tried to build friendships with classmates, but misunderstandings left me feeling discouraged.

name: Sad
description: Feeling down and heavy because of loss, hurt, or disappointment.
example: My favorite keychain went missing, and that made me feel sad.

name: Helpless
description: Feeling alone and unsupported, not knowing what to do.
example: When I ran into difficulties and could not find a solution, I felt helpless.

name: Sorrowful
description: Feeling deep sadness because of loss, pain, or something heartbreaking.
example: When my beloved pet passed away, I felt sorrowful and kept crying.

name: Heartbroken
description: Feeling emotional pain and hurt because of loss, sorrow, or betrayal.
example: I felt heartbroken after being betrayed by a close friend.

name: Depressed
description: A lasting emotional state of hopelessness, low mood, and lack of energy that can affect daily life.
example: The pressure of school that I could not release made me feel depressed and unmotivated.

name: Overwhelmed
description: Losing emotional control because of heavy pressure, pain, or difficulty.
example: Facing adults’ high expectations for a long time made me feel overwhelmed and drained.

name: Desperate
description: Feeling extremely low and completely without hope because of difficulty or repeated hurt.
example: After being lied to by my friend again and again, I felt desperate about the friendship.

name: Bored
description: Feeling unstimulated and uninterested in what is around you.
example: Because I could not go out on a rainy day, I felt bored inside.

name: Fed Up
description: Feeling mentally and physically tired of something repeated for too long.
example: Practicing the same thing again and again for a long time made me feel fed up.

name: Inferior
description: Feeling that your ability or worth is not enough, which can lead to insecurity and low confidence.
example: I feel inferior and ashamed when I think about my flaws and body shape.

name: Displeased
description: Feeling dislike, dissatisfaction, or discomfort toward something.
example: When I am forced to do something I do not like, I feel displeased and unwilling.

name: Jealous
description: Feeling envious and unhappy about what someone else has, achieves, or can do.
example: I felt jealous of other people’s talent and ability, and it made me feel smaller.

name: Disgusted
description: Feeling strong dislike and revulsion toward something.
example: I felt deeply disgusted by those awful behaviors.

name: Repulsed
description: Feeling disgust so strongly that it becomes hard to tolerate.
example: His two-faced behavior was so repulsive that I could hardly stand it.

name: Resentful
description: Feeling anger, bitterness, and blame toward a person or situation.
example: Being betrayed by someone I trusted made me feel deeply resentful.

name: Dissatisfied
description: Feeling unhappy with the current situation and wishing it were better.
example: I feel dissatisfied with how much adults restrict me.

name: Irritable
description: Feeling emotionally unsettled, impatient, or easily angered.
example: My younger brother kept making noise, and I felt irritable.

name: Angry
description: Feeling upset and angry after being provoked or bothered.
example: He kept interrupting my homework, and it made me angry.

name: Furious
description: A strong form of anger caused by frustration, unfairness, or deep dissatisfaction.
example: I felt furious when I was treated unfairly.

name: Enraged
description: An extreme form of anger that can lead to intense outbursts or loss of control.
example: Xiao-ming broke his promise again, and I became enraged and shouted at him on the phone.

name: Relieved
description: Letting go of past feelings or worries and finding emotional release and comfort.
example: After failing, I practiced accepting it and slowly felt relieved enough to start again.

name: Embarrassed
description: Feeling awkward and unsure of yourself in a sudden social or situational moment.
example: Introducing myself in front of strangers made me feel embarrassed.

name: Envious
description: Wanting something that someone else has and wishing you could have it too.
example: I envy his musical talent and wish I had that kind of ability too.

name: Guilty
description: Feeling uneasy or remorseful about something you did or chose.
example: I told a lie, and now I feel very guilty.

name: Conflicted
description: Feeling inner conflict between two or more thoughts, feelings, or choices.
example: I like sweets, but I also worry about my health, so I feel conflicted.

name: Regretful
description: Feeling sorry about a past decision, action, or missed opportunity.
example: I regret saying those words in front of my friend.

name: Wronged
description: Feeling hurt because you were treated unfairly or misunderstood.
example: I felt wronged because people misunderstood me and still blamed me.

name: Lonely
description: Feeling alone and separated from others.
example: I did not make any friends at my new school, so I felt lonely.

name: Ashamed
description: Feeling embarrassed or bad about your behavior, appearance, or words.
example: After making a small mistake and hearing classmates laugh, I felt embarrassed and ashamed.

name: Moved
description: Feeling deeply touched by a person, scene, or event.
example: My friend carefully prepared a birthday gift for me, and I was deeply moved.

name: Agitated
description: An excited and stirred-up emotional state, often caused by anticipation, excitement, or nervousness.
example: When I ran into my idol while shopping, I felt agitated with excitement and rushed over for a photo.
